NKJV.1: So He got into a boat, crossed over, and came to His own city.
NKJV.2: Then behold, they brought to Him a paralytic lying on a bed. When Jesus saw their faith, He said to the paralytic, “Son, be of good cheer; your sins are forgiven you.”
NKJV.3: And at once some of the scribes said within themselves, “This Man blasphemes!”
NKJV.4: But Jesus, knowing their thoughts, said, “Why do you think evil in your hearts?
NKJV.5: For which is easier, to say, ‘Your sins are forgiven you,’ or to say, ‘Arise and walk’?
NKJV.6: But that you may know that the Son of Man has power on earth to forgive sins”—then He said to the paralytic, “Arise, take up your bed, and go to your house.”
NKJV.7: And he arose and departed to his house.
NKJV.8: Now when the multitudes saw it, they marveled and glorified God, who had given such power to men.
NKJV.9: As Jesus passed on from there, He saw a man named Matthew sitting at the tax office. And He said to him, “Follow Me.” So he arose and followed Him.
NKJV.10: Now it happened, as Jesus sat at the table in the house, that behold, many tax collectors and sinners came and sat down with Him and His disciples.
NKJV.11: And when the Pharisees saw it, they said to His disciples, “Why does your Teacher eat with tax collectors and sinners?”
NKJV.12: When Jesus heard that, He said to them, “Those who are well have no need of a physician, but those who are sick.
NKJV.13: But go and learn what this means: ‘I desire mercy and not sacrifice.’ For I did not come to call the righteous, but sinners, to repentance.”
NKJV.14: Then the disciples of John came to Him, saying, “Why do we and the Pharisees fast often, but Your disciples do not fast?”
NKJV.15: And Jesus said to them, “Can  the friends of the bridegroom mourn as long as the bridegroom is with them? But the days will come when the bridegroom will be taken away from them, and  then they will fast.
NKJV.16: No one puts a piece of unshrunk cloth on an old garment; for the patch pulls away from the garment, and the tear is made worse.
NKJV.17: Nor do they put new wine into old wineskins, or else the wineskins break, the wine is spilled, and the wineskins are ruined. But they put new wine into new wineskins, and both are preserved.”
NKJV.18: While He spoke these things to them, behold, a ruler came and worshiped Him, saying, “My daughter has just died, but come and lay Your hand on her and she will live.”
NKJV.19: So Jesus arose and followed him, and so did His disciples.
NKJV.20: And suddenly, a woman who had a flow of blood for twelve years came from behind and touched the hem of His garment.
NKJV.21: For she said to herself, “If only I may touch His garment, I shall be made well.”
NKJV.22: But Jesus turned around, and when He saw her He said, “Be of good cheer, daughter;  your faith has made you well.” And the woman was made well from that hour.
NKJV.23: When Jesus came into the ruler’s house, and saw the flute players and the noisy crowd wailing,
NKJV.24: He said to them, “Make room, for the girl is not dead, but sleeping.” And they ridiculed Him.
NKJV.25: But when the crowd was put outside, He went in and took her by the hand, and the girl arose.
NKJV.26: And the report of this went out into all that land.
NKJV.27: When Jesus departed from there, two blind men followed Him, crying out and saying, “Son of David, have mercy on us!”
NKJV.28: And when He had come into the house, the blind men came to Him. And Jesus said to them, “Do you believe that I am able to do this?” They said to Him, “Yes, Lord.”
NKJV.29: Then He touched their eyes, saying, “According to your faith let it be to you.”
NKJV.30: And their eyes were opened. And Jesus sternly warned them, saying, “See that no one knows it.”
NKJV.31: But when they had departed, they spread the news about Him in all that country.
NKJV.32: As they went out, behold, they brought to Him a man, mute and demon-possessed.
NKJV.33: And when the demon was cast out, the mute spoke. And the multitudes marveled, saying, “It was never seen like this in Israel!”
NKJV.34: But the Pharisees said, “He casts out demons by the ruler of the demons.”
NKJV.35: Then Jesus went about all the cities and villages, teaching in their synagogues, preaching the gospel of the kingdom, and healing every sickness and every disease among the people.
NKJV.36: But when He saw the multitudes, He was moved with compassion for them, because they were weary and scattered, like sheep having no shepherd.
NKJV.37: Then He said to His disciples, “The harvest truly is plentiful, but the laborers are few.
NKJV.38: Therefore pray the Lord of the harvest to send out laborers into His harvest.”

ESV.1: And getting into a boat he crossed over and came to his own city.
ESV.2: And behold, some people brought to him a paralytic, lying on a bed. And when Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralytic,  “Take heart, my son; your sins are forgiven.”
ESV.3: And behold, some of the scribes said to themselves, “This man is blaspheming.”
ESV.4: But Jesus, knowing  their thoughts, said, “Why do you think evil in your hearts?
ESV.5: For which is easier, to say, ‘Your sins are forgiven,’ or to say, ‘Rise and walk’?
ESV.6: But that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins” — he then said to the paralytic — “Rise, pick up your bed and go home.”
ESV.7: And he rose and went home.
ESV.8: When the crowds saw it, they were afraid, and they glorified God, who had given such authority to men.
ESV.9: As Jesus passed on from there, he saw a man called Matthew sitting at the tax booth, and he said to him, “Follow me.” And he rose and followed him.
ESV.10: And as Jesus  reclined at table in the house, behold, many tax collectors and sinners came and were reclining with Jesus and his disciples.
ESV.11: And when the Pharisees saw this, they said to his disciples, “Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and sinners?”
ESV.12: But when he heard it, he said, “Those who are well have no need of a physician, but those who are sick.
ESV.13: Go and learn what this means: ‘I desire mercy, and not sacrifice.’ For I came not to call the righteous, but sinners.”
ESV.14: Then the disciples of John came to him, saying, “Why do we and the Pharisees fast,  but your disciples do not fast?”
ESV.15: And Jesus said to them,  “Can the wedding guests mourn as long as the bridegroom is with them? The days will come when the bridegroom is taken away from them, and then they will fast.
ESV.16: No one puts a piece of unshrunk cloth on an old garment, for the patch tears away from the garment, and a worse tear is made.
ESV.17: Neither is new wine put into old wineskins. If it is, the skins burst and the wine is spilled and the skins are destroyed. But new wine is put into fresh wineskins, and so both are preserved.”
ESV.18: While he was saying these things to them, behold, a ruler came in and knelt before him, saying, “My daughter has just died, but come and lay your hand on her, and she will live.”
ESV.19: And Jesus rose and followed him, with his disciples.
ESV.20: And behold, a woman who had suffered from a discharge of blood for twelve years came up behind him and touched the fringe of his garment,
ESV.21: for she said to herself, “If I only touch his garment, I will be made well.”
ESV.22: Jesus turned, and seeing her he said,  “Take heart, daughter; your faith has made you well.” And instantly  the woman was made well.
ESV.23: And when Jesus came to the ruler's house and saw the flute players and the crowd making a commotion,
ESV.24: he said, “Go away, for the girl is not dead but sleeping.” And they laughed at him.
ESV.25: But when the crowd had been put outside, he went in and took her by the hand, and the girl arose.
ESV.26: And the report of this went through all that district.
ESV.27: And as Jesus passed on from there, two blind men followed him, crying aloud, “Have mercy on us, Son of David.”
ESV.28: When he entered the house, the blind men came to him, and Jesus said to them, “Do you believe that I am able to do this?” They said to him, “Yes, Lord.”
ESV.29: Then he touched their eyes, saying,  “According to your faith be it done to you.”
ESV.30: And their eyes were opened. And Jesus sternly warned them,  “See that no one knows about it.”
ESV.31: But they went away and spread his fame through all that district.
ESV.32: As they were going away, behold, a demon-oppressed man who was mute was brought to him.
ESV.33: And when the demon had been cast out, the mute man spoke. And the crowds marveled, saying, “Never was anything like this seen in Israel.”
ESV.34: But the Pharisees said, “He casts out demons by the prince of demons.”

NLT.1: Jesus climbed into a boat and went back across the lake to his own town.
NLT.2: Some people brought to him a paralyzed man on a mat. Seeing their faith, Jesus said to the paralyzed man, “Be encouraged, my child! Your sins are forgiven.”
NLT.3: But some of the teachers of religious law said to themselves, “That’s blasphemy! Does he think he’s God?”
NLT.4: Jesus knew what they were thinking, so he asked them, “Why do you have such evil thoughts in your hearts?
NLT.5: Is it easier to say ‘Your sins are forgiven,’ or ‘Stand up and walk’?
NLT.6: So I will prove to you that the Son of Man has the authority on earth to forgive sins.” Then Jesus turned to the paralyzed man and said, “Stand up, pick up your mat, and go home!”
NLT.7: And the man jumped up and went home!
NLT.8: Fear swept through the crowd as they saw this happen. And they praised God for giving humans such authority.
NLT.9: As Jesus was walking along, he saw a man named Matthew sitting at his tax collector’s booth. “Follow me and be my disciple,” Jesus said to him. So Matthew got up and followed him.
NLT.10: Later, Matthew invited Jesus and his disciples to his home as dinner guests, along with many tax collectors and other disreputable sinners.
NLT.11: But when the Pharisees saw this, they asked his disciples, “Why does your teacher eat with such scum? ”
NLT.12: When Jesus heard this, he said, “Healthy people don’t need a doctor — sick people do.”
NLT.13: Then he added, “Now go and learn the meaning of this Scripture: ‘I want you to show mercy, not offer sacrifices.’ For I have come to call not those who think they are righteous, but those who know they are sinners.”
NLT.14: One day the disciples of John the Baptist came to Jesus and asked him, “Why don’t your disciples fast like we do and the Pharisees do?”
NLT.15: Jesus replied, “Do wedding guests mourn while celebrating with the groom? Of course not. But someday the groom will be taken away from them, and then they will fast.
NLT.16: “Besides, who would patch old clothing with new cloth? For the new patch would shrink and rip away from the old cloth, leaving an even bigger tear than before.
NLT.17: “And no one puts new wine into old wineskins. For the old skins would burst from the pressure, spilling the wine and ruining the skins. New wine is stored in new wineskins so that both are preserved.”
NLT.18: As Jesus was saying this, the leader of a synagogue came and knelt before him. “My daughter has just died,” he said, “but you can bring her back to life again if you just come and lay your hand on her.”
NLT.19: So Jesus and his disciples got up and went with him.
NLT.20: Just then a woman who had suffered for twelve years with constant bleeding came up behind him. She touched the fringe of his robe,
NLT.21: for she thought, “If I can just touch his robe, I will be healed.”
NLT.22: Jesus turned around, and when he saw her he said, “Daughter, be encouraged! Your faith has made you well.” And the woman was healed at that moment.
NLT.23: When Jesus arrived at the official’s home, he saw the noisy crowd and heard the funeral music.
NLT.24: “Get out!” he told them. “The girl isn’t dead; she’s only asleep.” But the crowd laughed at him.
NLT.25: After the crowd was put outside, however, Jesus went in and took the girl by the hand, and she stood up!
NLT.26: The report of this miracle swept through the entire countryside.
NLT.27: After Jesus left the girl’s home, two blind men followed along behind him, shouting, “Son of David, have mercy on us!”
NLT.28: They went right into the house where he was staying, and Jesus asked them, “Do you believe I can make you see?” “Yes, Lord,” they told him, “we do.”
NLT.29: Then he touched their eyes and said, “Because of your faith, it will happen.”
NLT.30: Then their eyes were opened, and they could see! Jesus sternly warned them, “Don’t tell anyone about this.”
NLT.31: But instead, they went out and spread his fame all over the region.
NLT.32: When they left, a demon-possessed man who couldn’t speak was brought to Jesus.
NLT.33: So Jesus cast out the demon, and then the man began to speak. The crowds were amazed. “Nothing like this has ever happened in Israel!” they exclaimed.
NLT.34: But the Pharisees said, “He can cast out demons because he is empowered by the prince of demons.”
NLT.35: Jesus traveled through all the towns and villages of that area, teaching in the synagogues and announcing the Good News about the Kingdom. And he healed every kind of disease and illness.
NLT.36: When he saw the crowds, he had compassion on them because they were confused and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.
NLT.37: He said to his disciples, “The harvest is great, but the workers are few.
NLT.38: So pray to the Lord who is in charge of the harvest; ask him to send more workers into his fields.”

AMP.1: And Jesus, getting into a boat, crossed over the Sea of Galilee and came to [Capernaum] His own city.
AMP.2: They brought to Him a man who was paralyzed, lying on a stretcher. Seeing their [active] faith [springing from confidence in Him], Jesus said to the paralytic, "Do not be afraid, son; your sins are forgiven [the penalty is paid, the guilt removed, and you are declared to be in right standing with God]."
AMP.3: And some of the scribes said to themselves, "This man blasphemes [by claiming the rights and prerogatives of God]!"
AMP.4: But Jesus, knowing their thoughts, said, "Why do you think evil in your hearts?
AMP.5: For which is easier, to say, 'Your sins are forgiven and the penalty paid,' or to say, 'Get up and walk'? [Both are possible for God; both are impossible for man.]
AMP.6: But so that you may know that the Son of Man has authority and the power on earth to forgive sins"—then He said to the paralytic, "Get up, pick up your stretcher and go home."
AMP.7: And he got up and went home [healed and forgiven].
AMP.8: When the crowds saw this, they were awestruck, and glorified God and praised Him, who had given such authority and power to men.
AMP.9: As Jesus went on from there, He saw a man named Matthew (Levi) sitting in the tax collector's booth; and He said to him, "Follow Me [as My disciple, accepting Me as your Master and Teacher and walking the same path of life that I walk]." And Matthew got up and followed Him.
AMP.10: Then as Jesus was reclining at the table in Matthew's house, many tax collectors and sinners [including non-observant Jews] came and ate with Him and His disciples.
AMP.11: When the Pharisees saw this, they asked His disciples, "Why does your Master eat with tax collectors and sinners?"
AMP.12: But when Jesus heard this, He said, "Those who are healthy have no need for a physician, but [only] those who are sick.
AMP.13: Go and learn what this [Scripture] means: 'I desire compassion [for those in distress], and not [animal] sacrifice,' for I did not come to call [to repentance] the [self-proclaimed] righteous [who see no need to change], but sinners [those who recognize their sin and actively seek forgiveness]."
AMP.14: Then the disciples of John [the Baptist] came to Jesus, asking, "Why do we and the Pharisees often fast [as a religious exercise], but Your disciples do not fast?"
AMP.15: And Jesus replied to them, "Can the guests of the bridegroom mourn while the bridegroom is with them? The days will come when the bridegroom is taken away from them, and then they will fast.
AMP.16: But no one puts a piece of unshrunk (new) cloth on an old garment; for the patch pulls away from the garment, and a worse tear results.
AMP.17: Nor is new wine put into old wineskins [that have lost their elasticity]; otherwise the wineskins burst, and the [fermenting] wine spills and the wineskins are ruined. But new wine is put into fresh wineskins, so both are preserved."
AMP.18: While He was saying these things to them, a ruler (synagogue official) entered [the house] and kneeled down and worshiped Him, saying, "My daughter has just now died; but come and lay Your hand on her, and she will live."
AMP.19: Jesus got up and began to accompany the ruler, with His disciples.
AMP.20: Then a woman who had suffered from a hemorrhage for twelve years came up behind Him and touched the [tassel] fringe of His outer robe;
AMP.21: for she had been saying to herself, "If I only touch His outer robe, I will be healed."
AMP.22: But Jesus turning and seeing her said, "Take courage, daughter; your [personal trust and confident] faith [in Me] has made you well." And at once the woman was [completely] healed.
AMP.23: When Jesus came to the ruler's house, and saw the flute players [who were professional, hired mourners] and the [grieving] crowd making an uproar,
AMP.24: He said, "Go away; for the girl is not dead, but is sleeping." And they laughed and jeered at Him.
AMP.25: But when the crowd had been sent outside, Jesus went in and took her by the hand, and the girl got up.
AMP.26: And the news about this spread throughout all that district.
AMP.27: As Jesus went on from there, two blind men followed Him, screaming loudly, "Have mercy and compassion on us, Son of David (Messiah)!"
AMP.28: When He went into the house, the blind men came up to Him, and Jesus said to them, "Do you believe [with a deep, abiding trust] that I am able to do this?" They said to Him, "Yes, Lord."
AMP.29: Then He touched their eyes, saying, "According to your faith [your trust and confidence in My power and My ability to heal] it will be done to you."
AMP.30: And their eyes were opened. And Jesus sternly warned them: "See that no one knows this!"
AMP.31: But they went out and spread the news about Him throughout that whole district.
AMP.32: While they were going away, a mute, demon-possessed man was brought to Jesus.
AMP.33: And when the demon was driven out [by Jesus], the mute man spoke; and the crowds wondered in amazement, saying, "Never before has anything like this [miracle] been seen in Israel."
AMP.34: But the Pharisees were saying, "He casts out the demons by [the power of] the ruler of demons."
AMP.35: Jesus went throughout all the cities and villages [in Galilee], teaching in their synagogues and proclaiming the good news (gospel) of the kingdom, and healing every kind of disease and every kind of sickness [His words and His works reflecting His Messiahship].
AMP.36: When He saw the crowds, He was moved with compassion and pity for them, because they were dispirited and distressed, like sheep without a shepherd.
AMP.37: Then He said to His disciples, "The harvest is [indeed] plentiful, but the workers are few.
AMP.38: So pray to the Lord of the harvest to send out workers into His harvest."

MSG.1: Back in the boat, Jesus and the disciples recrossed the sea to Jesus' hometown.
MSG.2: They were hardly out of the boat when some men carried a paraplegic on a stretcher and set him down in front of them. Jesus, impressed by their bold belief, said to the paraplegic, "Cheer up, son. I forgive your sins."
MSG.3: Some religion scholars whispered, "Why, that's blasphemy!"
MSG.4: Jesus knew what they were thinking, and said, "Why this gossipy whispering?
MSG.5: Which do you think is simpler: to say, 'I forgive your sins,' or, 'Get up and walk'?
MSG.6: Well, just so it's clear that I'm the Son of Man and authorized to do either, or both... ." At this he turned to the paraplegic and said, "Get up. Take your bed and go home."
MSG.8: The crowd was awestruck, amazed and pleased that God had authorized Jesus to work among them this way.
MSG.9: Passing along, Jesus saw a man at his work collecting taxes. His name was Matthew. Jesus said, "Come along with me." Matthew stood up and followed him.
MSG.10: Later when Jesus was eating supper at Matthew's house with his close followers, a lot of disreputable characters came and joined them.
MSG.11: When the Pharisees saw him keeping this kind of company, they had a fit, and lit into Jesus' followers. "What kind of example is this from your Teacher, acting cozy with crooks and riff-raff?"
MSG.12: Jesus, overhearing, shot back, "Who needs a doctor: the healthy or the sick?
MSG.13: Go figure out what this Scripture means: 'I'm after mercy, not religion.' I'm here to invite outsiders, not coddle insiders."
MSG.14: A little later John's followers approached, asking, "Why is it that we and the Pharisees rigorously discipline body and spirit by fasting, but your followers don't?"
MSG.15: Jesus told them, "When you're celebrating a wedding, you don't skimp on the cake and wine. You feast. Later you may need to pull in your belt, but not now. No one throws cold water on a friendly bonfire. This is Kingdom Come!"
MSG.16: He went on, "No one cuts up a fine silk scarf to patch old work clothes; you want fabrics that match.
MSG.17: And you don't put your wine in cracked bottles."
MSG.18: As he finished saying this, a local official appeared, bowed politely, and said, "My daughter has just now died. If you come and touch her, she will live."
MSG.19: Jesus got up and went with him, his disciples following along.
MSG.20: Just then a woman who had hemorrhaged for twelve years slipped in from behind and lightly touched his robe.
MSG.21: She was thinking to herself, "If I can just put a finger on his robe, I'll get well." Jesus turned—caught her at it. Then he reassured her: "Courage, daughter. You took a risk of faith, and now you're well."
MSG.22: The woman was well from then on.
MSG.23: By now they had arrived at the house of the town official, and pushed their way through the gossips looking for a story and the neighbors bringing in casseroles.

NIV.1:
Jesus Heals a Paralytic
Jesus stepped into a boat, crossed over and came to his own town.
NIV.2: Some men brought to him a paralytic, lying on a mat. When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralytic, “Take heart, son; your sins are forgiven.”
NIV.3: At this, some of the teachers of the law said to themselves, “This fellow is blaspheming!”
NIV.4: Knowing their thoughts, Jesus said, “Why do you entertain evil thoughts in your hearts?
NIV.5: Which is easier: to say, ‘Your sins are forgiven,’ or to say, ‘Get up and walk’?
NIV.6: But so that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins…” Then he said to the paralytic, “Get up, take your mat and go home.”
NIV.7: And the man got up and went home.
NIV.8: When the crowd saw this, they were filled with awe; and they praised God, who had given such authority to men.
NIV.9:
The Calling of Matthew
As Jesus went on from there, he saw a man named Matthew sitting at the tax collector’s booth. “Follow me,” he told him, and Matthew got up and followed him.
NIV.10: While Jesus was having dinner at Matthew’s house, many tax collectors and “sinners” came and ate with him and his disciples.
NIV.11: When the Pharisees saw this, they asked his disciples, “Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and ‘sinners’?”
NIV.12: On hearing this, Jesus said, “It is not the healthy who need a doctor, but the sick.
NIV.13: But go and learn what this means: ‘I desire mercy, not sacrifice.’ For I have not come to call the righteous, but sinners.”
NIV.14:
Jesus Questioned About Fasting
Then John’s disciples came and asked him, “How is it that we and the Pharisees fast, but your disciples do not fast?”
NIV.15: Jesus answered, “How can the guests of the bridegroom mourn while he is with them? The time will come when the bridegroom will be taken from them; then they will fast.
NIV.16: “No one sews a patch of unshrunk cloth on an old garment, for the patch will pull away from the garment, making the tear worse.
NIV.17: Neither do men pour new wine into old wineskins. If they do, the skins will burst, the wine will run out and the wineskins will be ruined. No, they pour new wine into new wineskins, and both are preserved.”
NIV.18:
A Dead Girl and a Sick Woman
While he was saying this, a ruler came and knelt before him and said, “My daughter has just died. But come and put your hand on her, and she will live.”
NIV.19: Jesus got up and went with him, and so did his disciples.
NIV.20: Just then a woman who had been subject to bleeding for twelve years came up behind him and touched the edge of his cloak.
NIV.21: She said to herself, “If I only touch his cloak, I will be healed.”
NIV.22: Jesus turned and saw her. “Take heart, daughter,” he said, “your faith has healed you.” And the woman was healed from that moment.
NIV.23: When Jesus entered the ruler’s house and saw the flute players and the noisy crowd,
NIV.24: he said, “Go away. The girl is not dead but asleep.” But they laughed at him.
NIV.25: After the crowd had been put outside, he went in and took the girl by the hand, and she got up.
NIV.26: News of this spread through all that region.
NIV.27:
Jesus Heals the Blind and Mute
As Jesus went on from there, two blind men followed him, calling out, “Have mercy on us, Son of David!”
NIV.28:
When he had gone indoors, the blind men came to him, and he asked them, “Do you believe that I am able to do this?”
“Yes, Lord,” they replied.
NIV.29: Then he touched their eyes and said, “According to your faith will it be done to you”;
NIV.30: and their sight was restored. Jesus warned them sternly, “See that no one knows about this.”
NIV.31: But they went out and spread the news about him all over that region.
NIV.32: While they were going out, a man who was demon-possessed and could not talk was brought to Jesus.
NIV.33: And when the demon was driven out, the man who had been mute spoke. The crowd was amazed and said, “Nothing like this has ever been seen in Israel.”
NIV.34: But the Pharisees said, “It is by the prince of demons that he drives out demons.”
NIV.35:
The Workers Are Few
Jesus went through all the towns and villages, teaching in their synagogues, preaching the good news of the kingdom and healing every disease and sickness.
NIV.36: When he saw the crowds, he had compassion on them, because they were harassed and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.
NIV.37: Then he said to his disciples, “The harvest is plentiful but the workers are few.
NIV.38: Ask the Lord of the harvest, therefore, to send out workers into his harvest field.”
